takeaways: Senior living occupancy approaching 91-92% by late 2026; discounts vanishing as capital returns to sector
Commercial mortgage rates stable at 5.19-8.75%; capital markets recovery on track with 15-20% sales activity increase
AI proptech funding surges to $4.5B in 2025; agentic AI emerging as next frontier for real estate automation

items.headline: Senior Living Discounts Disappearing as Demand Surge Accelerates Consolidation
items.summary: Senior living operators are experiencing unprecedented demand with occupancy levels climbing toward 91-92% by late 2026. With rental discounts vanishing and capital returning to the sector, large portfolios are changing hands as operators split properties into smaller pieces. Senior living providers are shifting to granular rate-setting strategies by unit type to maximize revenue, marking a sophisticated evolution beyond macro-level pricing moves.

items.headline: CRE Capital Markets Show Strong Recovery; Rates Stable, Sales Activity Up 15-20%
items.summary: Commercial mortgage rates have stabilized at 5.19-8.75% as of March 22, 2026, with the 10-year Treasury yield around 4.26%. Colliers forecasts a 15-20% increase in sales activity for 2026 as institutional and cross-border capital returns. Approximately 83% of global CRE leaders now expect revenue growth by end of 2026, with lenders showing increased confidence in strong cash flow deals.

items.headline: AI Proptech Funding Reaches $4.5B; Agentic AI Moves From Experimentation to Delivery
items.summary: Venture capital investment in AI proptech reached $4.5 billion in 2025, representing the fastest-growing segment within the $16.7B total proptech funding boom. Agentic AI is emerging as the next phase, automating complex workflows across procurement, pricing, and inventory management. However, trust remains a barrier, with only 27% of firms expressing confidence in AI for financial underwriting.

items.headline: Erickson Starts Maryland Community; National Church Residences Renovates Texas
items.summary: Senior housing developers continue momentum with Erickson breaking ground on a new Maryland senior living community and National Church Residences underway with renovations of its Texas property. These projects reflect growing confidence in the senior housing market as development pipelines, though still historically thin, accelerate across major markets.

items.headline: Hayes Barton Place in Raleigh Achieves Full Occupancy; Walkable CCRC Model Proves Successful
items.summary: Hayes Barton Place, a 90-unit continuing care retirement community in Raleigh, North Carolina, achieved full occupancy by January 2026. The community's success demonstrates strong market demand for premium senior living that combines nostalgic charm with modern amenities and walkable connectivity to sought-after local destinations.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady; Long-Term CRE Financing Environment Remains Constructive
items.summary: The Federal Reserve maintained the federal funds rate at 3.50-3.75% on January 28, 2026, supporting a more constructive environment for CRE financing. Transactions with strong cash flow and conservative leverage continue to close, while marginal deals remain difficult. Lenders remain selective despite improving capital access.

items.headline: 71% of Leaders Under Increased Stress; CEO AI Strategy Ownership Doubles Year-Over-Year
items.summary: McKinsey research shows 71% of leaders are under increased stress as 40% consider leaving their jobs amid record U.S. layoffs. However, CEO ownership of AI strategy has doubled, with nearly three quarters of CEOs making primary AI decisions. Companies plan to double AI spending from 0.8% to 1.7% of revenues in 2026, with half of CEOs believing their job depends on AI ROI.

items.headline: Emotional Intelligence Emerges as Critical Leadership Competency in 2026
items.summary: As organizations navigate complexity without clear roadmaps, emotional intelligence is becoming the defining capacity for steady learning and execution. Leaders with high EI are better positioned to work through challenges collaboratively rather than retreating into defensive individual survival modes during periods of organizational change.

items.headline: Learning Becomes Core Talent Lever; Competition for Talent Shifts to Speed of Adoption
items.summary: The war for talent is no longer just about hiring—it's about organizational learning speed. Professionals increasingly expect growth opportunities as a core benefit, making learning infrastructure and strategic agility the primary levers for talent attraction and retention in 2026.

items.headline: Fibremaxxing Drives Workplace Productivity; Gut Health Linked to 17% Lower Absenteeism
items.summary: Fibremaxxing—intentional high-volume plant fiber consumption—is the new productivity standard for 2026. New data shows employees with healthy digestive systems report significantly fewer sick days, with some studies indicating 17% reduction in absenteeism among teams prioritizing gut-friendly diets. March National Nutrition Month offers ideal timing for corporate wellness initiatives.

items.headline: Global Wellness Summit Releases 10 Trends for 2026; Personalization and Prevention Lead
items.summary: The Global Wellness Institute identifies personalized wellness, emotional fitness, and backlash against over-optimization as defining trends for 2026. AI-driven biomarker testing and personalized health tracking are becoming mainstream, while emotional fitness emerges as a new health category emphasizing cognitive performance and emotional resilience.

items.headline: Thrive Senior Living Acquires Three Communities; AgeWell Living Rebrands Portfolio
items.summary: Recent dealbook activity shows Thrive Senior Living acquiring three senior housing communities while AgeWell Living pursues a portfolio rebranding strategy. These transactions reflect strengthened investor confidence and the consolidation trend driven by improving senior housing fundamentals and capital availability.
